title: IAM, Bucket Policies, Ownership, Access Points, and Public Access · Language Lens: Block Public Ownership
summary: A bucket is private by convention, one application role has s3:*, tenant prefixes are interpolated from user input, and ACLs conflict with ownership and public-block assumptions. Predict the representation, storage, trust, cost, and recovery evidence before inspecting a model. Planned artifact: Add and verify the block public ownership increment in least-privilege S3 authorization proof. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence…
objectives: Enforce Block Public Access and bucket-owner-enforced Object Ownership while explaining what each does and does not authorize.
Evaluate identity and resource policies by principal, action, exact resource, condition, session, explicit deny, and organization boundary.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: S3, CloudFront, and Go Predict Block Public Ownership · Theory Block Public Ownership
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory Go file-delivery team handles case 3307 during a compliance restore drill. Build deterministic Go evidence to prove explicit-deny-aware least privilege, public block, ownership, and tenant scope; retain stable tenant and file identity without exposing it in metric labels; then introduce a duplicate event or ambiguous response and reconcile without duplicate effect. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. AWS SDK packages, networks, S3, CloudFront, KMS, IAM, WAF, databases, credentials, host state, race and leak checks, load, faults, restore, billing, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Enforce Block Public Access and bucket-owner-enforced Object Ownership while explaining what each does and does not authorize.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Block Public Ownership contract for the supplied data.
